# Beudox HR Management System - Technical Context

## Architecture Overview

Beudox is a single-page application (SPA) built with React and TypeScript, using Vite as the build tool. The application follows a component-based architecture with clear separation of concerns.

### Frontend Architecture
- **Framework**: React 18 with functional components and hooks
- **Language**: TypeScript for type safety
- **Routing**: React Router DOM for client-side routing
- **State Management**: TanStack Query for server state, React Context for global state
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with shadcn/ui component library
- **Build Tool**: Vite for fast development and optimized production builds

### Backend Architecture
- **Platform**: Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Edge Functions)
- **Database**: PostgreSQL with Row Level Security (RLS)
- **Authentication**: Supabase Auth with role-based access control
- **API**: Supabase client for database operations and real-time subscriptions
- **Edge Functions**: Serverless functions for complex business logic (payroll, notifications, etc.)

## Key Components and Patterns

### Core Components
- **AppLayout**: Main application layout with sidebar navigation
- **AppSidebar**: Navigation sidebar with role-based menu items
- **TopBar**: Header with notifications and user menu
- **BeudoxLogo**: Logo component with multiple variants
- **NavLink**: Wrapper around React Router NavLink with active state styling

### Feature Components
- **SearchableEmployeeSelect**: Combobox for employee selection with search and avatars
- **EvaluationTimeline**: Timeline view of employee evaluations (quarterly and daily)
- **RichTextEditor**: WYSIWYG editor using Tiptap for HR policies
- **Leave Management**: Components for applying, viewing, and managing leave requests
- **Settings Components**: Configuration panels for departments, roles, leave types, etc.

### UI Component Library
The application uses shadcn/ui components built on Radix UI primitives:
- Form controls (Button, Input, Select, etc.)
- Layout components (Card, Tabs, Dialog, etc.)
- Data display (Table, Badge, Avatar, etc.)
- Feedback (Toast, Alert, Skeleton, etc.)

## Database Schema

### Core Tables
- **employees**: Employee profiles with roles, departments, contact info
- **companies**: Company information and settings
- **departments**: Organizational departments
- **roles**: User roles with permissions
- **leave_types**: Configurable leave types (vacation, sick, etc.)
- **leave_requests**: Employee leave requests with approval workflow
- **evaluations**: Quarterly performance evaluations
- **daily_evaluations**: Daily feedback and evaluations
- **expense_categories**: Categories for expense tracking
- **notifications**: System notifications

### Key Relationships
- Employees belong to companies and departments
- Evaluations link employees to evaluators
- Leave requests reference leave types and approval chains
- Daily evaluations have reviewer/reviewee relationships

## Business Logic

### Authentication & Authorization
- Role-based access control (CEO, HR Manager, Team Lead, Employee)
- Row Level Security policies in Supabase
- Permission checks in components and API calls

### Evaluation System
- **Quarterly Evaluations**: Formal performance reviews with scores and recommendations
- **Daily Evaluations**: Quick feedback with directional ratings
- Timeline view combining both evaluation types
- Visibility rules based on user roles and relationships

### Leave Management
- Configurable leave types with balance tracking
- Approval workflows based on organizational hierarchy
- Balance calculations considering working days

### Notification System
- Automated notifications for HR events
- Email sending via Supabase Edge Functions
- In-app notification bell with unread counts

## Key Files and Their Purposes

### Entrypoints
- `src/main.tsx`: Application bootstrap and React rendering
- `src/pages/Index.tsx`: Main page component (Next.js Pages Router pattern)

### Core Logic
- `src/hooks/useAuth.ts`: Authentication state management
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Permission checking utilities
- `src/lib/leave-utils.ts`: Leave balance and calculation functions
- `src/lib/notifications.ts`: Notification sending logic
- `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`: Supabase client configuration

### Configuration
- `vite.config.ts`: Vite build configuration
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Tailwind CSS configuration
- `supabase/config.toml`: Supabase project configuration
- `package.json`: Dependencies and scripts

## Development Workflow

### Code Organization
- Components are organized by feature (leave/, evaluations/, settings/)
- Shared utilities in lib/ directory
- TypeScript interfaces in integrations/supabase/types.ts
- Environment variables prefixed with VITE_ for client-side access

### Testing Strategy
- Unit tests for utilities and hooks
- Component tests for UI logic
- E2E tests with Playwright for critical user flows

### Deployment
- Built with Vite for optimized production bundles
- Static hosting compatible (Vercel, Netlify, etc.)
- Supabase handles backend scaling automatically

## Security Considerations

- Row Level Security enabled on all database tables
- Authentication required for all data access
- API keys stored as environment variables
- Client-side validation with server-side enforcement
- Role-based UI rendering to prevent unauthorized actions

## Performance Optimizations

- TanStack Query for efficient data fetching and caching
- Lazy loading of routes and components
- Optimized bundle splitting with Vite
- Database indexes on frequently queried columns
- Real-time subscriptions for live updates where needed
